name: AK-15
description: The AK-15 is essentially a version of the AK-12, chambered for 7.62x39mm ammunition. This ammunition has superior penetration and stopping power compared to 5.45x39mm ammunition, or standard 5.56x45mm ammunition. 7.62x39mm ammunition is still used by the military and law enforcement forces and is widely used around the world. Some operators prefer this caliber. There were also plans to chamber the AK-15 for standard 7.62x51mm ammunition. The new weapon is also better balanced and has improved ergonomics.

name: AK-47
description: The AK-47 or AK is a selective-fire (semi-automatic and automatic), gas-operated 7.62×39mm assault rifle. Even after almost seven decades, the model and its variants remain the most popular and widely used assault rifles in the world because of their substantial reliability under harsh conditions, with low production costs compared to contemporary Western weapons, availability in virtually every geographic region and ease of use.

name: AKM
description: The AKM is an assault rifle using the 7.62×39mm Soviet intermediate cartridge. It is gas operated with a rotating bolt. The AKM is capable of selective fire, firing either single shots or automatically at a cyclic rate of 600 rounds/min. Despite being replaced in the late 1970s by the AK-74, the AKM is still in service in some army reserve and second-line units and several East European countries.

name: AWM (Base)
description: Bolt action sniper rifle with removable box magazine. Uses .338 caliber ammo.

name: Block 21
description: The Block 21 is a full-size service pistol, a standard law enforcement round with proven knock-down power. The Block 21 is a short-recoil operated, striker-fire semi-automatic pistol. Uses: 0.45 ACP ammunition.

name: DEagle .357
description: The DEagle .357 is a semi-automatic handgun notable for chambering the largest centerfire cartridge of any magazine-fed, self-loading pistol. Uses .357 caliber ammunition.

name: DEagle 50 (Premium)
description: The DEagle 50 is a semi-automatic handgun notable for chambering the largest centerfire cartridge of any magazine-fed, self-loading pistol. Uses: .50 AE ammunition.

name: DEagle 50 (Gold)
description: The DEagle 50 is a semi-automatic handgun notable for chambering the largest centerfire cartridge of any magazine-fed, self-loading pistol. Uses: .50 AE ammunition.

name: Judge 44
description: A modified serpent, with a swapped cylinder and barrel to chamber .44 rounds. A custom grip for style that shows the hammer of justice that the Judge hands out.

name: Krueger
description: The Krueger is a rimfire semi-automatic pistol modeled and made after the Japanese Nambu pistols. Uses: .22 cal Ammunition.

name: M16A4
description: The M16A4 is the fourth generation of the M16 series. It has a full length rail system. Fires the 5.56x45 mm rounds.

description: Weapon_M1887_Sawed_off

name: M1911 (Base)
description: The M1911 pistol originated in the late 1890s as the result of a search for a suitable self-loading (or semi-automatic) pistol to replace the variety of revolvers then in service. It is a single-action, semi-automatic, magazine-fed, recoil-operated pistol chambered for the .45 ACP cartridge.

name: M1911 (Engraved)
description: The M1911 pistol originated in the late 1890s as the result of a search for a suitable self-loading (or semi-automatic) pistol to replace the variety of revolvers then in service. It is a single-action, semi-automatic, magazine-fed, recoil-operated pistol chambered for the .45 ACP cartridge.

name: M1911 (Gold)
description: The M1911 pistol originated in the late 1890s as the result of a search for a suitable self-loading (or semi-automatic) pistol to replace the variety of revolvers then in service. It is a single-action, semi-automatic, magazine-fed, recoil-operated pistol chambered for the .45 ACP cartridge.

name: M1911 (JayW)
description: The M1911 pistol originated in the late 1890s as the result of a search for a suitable self-loading (or semi-automatic) pistol to replace the variety of revolvers then in service. It is a single-action, semi-automatic, magazine-fed, recoil-operated pistol chambered for the .45 ACP cartridge.

name: M1 Rifle
description: The M1 rifle is a .30 caliber, gas-operated, 8 shot clip-fed, semi-automatic rifle designed in 1928 and it was built in more than 5 million pieces.

name: TEC01 M9
description: The M9 is a short recoil, semi-automatic, single-action / double-action pistol that uses a 15-round staggered box magazine. The TEC01 M9 was manufactured for the TEC 01 company. Uses: 9mm ammunition.

name: MP5 K
description: The MP5K is a 9mm submachine gun, developed in the 1960s. The MP5K is one of the most widely used submachine guns in the world, having been adopted by 40 nations and numerous military, law enforcement, intelligence, and security organizations. This is a shorter version of the MP5 with no stock. It uses 9mm ammunition.

name: MP5 SD
description: The MP5 SD is a 9mm submachine gun, developed in the 1960s. The MP5 SD is one of the most widely used submachine guns in the world, having been adopted by 40 nations and numerous military, law enforcement, intelligence, and security organizations. This version of the MP5 features an integrated suppressor and A2 fixed stock. Uses: 9mm ammunition.

name: Peacekeeper 38
description: A Peacekeeper is a double action revolver chambered in a .38 special, although it can also use .357. Developed at the start of the 20th century it was quickly adapted as a favorite weapon of law enforcement agencies around the world. Simple to maintain, easy to shoot and having that classic look, it is still a preferred sidearm of many.

name: RPK
description: The RPK is a selective-fire (semi-automatic and automatic), gas-operated 7.62×39mm light machine gun. The RPK features a thicker and longer barrel than the AKM. This enables it to be fired for longer without permanent loss in accuracy due to the barrel heating up. The main feature of the rifle is that it can take both regular AK magazines and its own 75-rnd drum magazine. The RPK entered service in early 1960s and can still be seen in action to this day.

name: SCAR-DMR
description: The SCAR 17S DMR mod. is a Belgium-manufactured gas-operated, short-stroke gas piston semi-automatic marksman rifle. Uses .308 caliber ammo.

name: SDASS 12M
description: The SDASS 12M Compact (often called BDASS) is a light pump-action shotgun. Its impressive damage and fire rate make it perfect for close combat action. It uses 12 gauge shotgun shells and it can fit up to 7 shells.

name: Serpent 357
description: The iconic revolver developed during the 1950s. This premium double action revolver is chambered in .357 magnum caliber, although it can also use .38. It is world-renowned for its quality, accuracy, stability, smooth trigger pull and a tight cylinder lockup.

name: HS SF19
description: SF19 offers everything you need in full size, high capacity strike fired pistol. Featuring forged slide and hammer forged barrel with Melonite protective finish. Redesign slide has deeper, more pronounced front and rear serrations for easier manipulation. Uses 9mm ammo.

name: SVD
description: The SVD sniper rifle is a semi-automatic sniper / designated marksman rifle chambered in 7.62×54mmR. The SVD was designed as a squad support weapon.

name: VHS 2
description: VHS-2 is a fully ambidextrous 5.56 NATO caliber assault rifle. Designed on a bullpup modular platform, it maintains all the ballistic benefits of a longer barrel in a shorter package without sacrificing overall length, accuracy and muzzle velocity. The rifle is equipped with a carrying handle with a NATO STANAG 4694 rail and flip-up iron sights. Uses 5.56x45mm ammo.

name: VSS VZ
description: Factory silenced sniper rifle. Uses 9x39mm ammunition.
